## Overview
D long stay (National) visa for applicants with study exchange or voluntary work agreement.
If the intended stay in Latvia is less than 90 days in 180 day period, applicant shall apply for C short stay (Schengen) visa.
Eligibility
Applicant must meet the following criteria in order to be eligible to submit a Schengen visa application:
- holding a valid passport/travel document;
- holding a valid Egyptian visa or residence permit in case of non-Egyptians;
- applying for a D long stay (National) visa;
- must be citizens or valid residence permit holders (studies, employment or family member) of countries of the territorial competence of the Embassy of Latvia to Egypt:
 
Noteikumi par Latvijas Republikas diplomātisko un konsulāro pārstāvniecību teritoriālo kompetenci vīzu pieprasīšanai. (https://m.likumi.lv/ta/en/en/id/214846)

## Visa Fees
Visa Fees applicable with effect from 01 January 2025 for residence permit visa in Egypt is:

[Study Exchange or Volunteering]
The above mentioned Visa Fee should be paid in cash, credit card and debit card.
- VFS service fee of EGP 1874 (VAT tax included) is charged per visa application, in addition to the applicable visa fee.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required

- The list of documents below is not an exhaustive list of documents and the applicant can always be asked to provide additional information/documents, if requested by the Embassy.
- Both the standard and the supporting documentation have to be recent, i.e. not older than 1 month, and need to be provided for every Schengen visa application.
- Applicants always have to bring and present their original documents, but they only need to submit photocopies of the same, unless the requirements specify that an original document has to be submitted.
- If the applicant is under the age of 18, one of the Parents must at least accompany the minor during the visit at VFS Center, and the other parent must provide an original official (Notarized) consent letter in both English and Arabic. also, if one of the applicants is living abroad must provide an original official (Notarized) consent letter in both English and Arabic. please note that original birth certificate translated into English must be provided and provide copies from parent's passports.
- Documents to be provided by all visa applicants must be in English.
- If the applicant is under the age of 18, one of the Parents must at least accompany the minor during the visit at VFS Center, and the other parent must provide an original official (Notarized) consent letter in both English and Arabic. also, if one of the applicants is living abroad must provide an original official (Notarized) consent letter in both English and Arabic. please note that original birth certificate translated into English must be provided and provide copies from parent's passports.
- The list of documents is available here:
 
CHECKLIST
Please note:
Only applications with all of the supporting documents will be accepted.
Each applicant must present a separate application form and set of the supporting documents.
For Latvia, every applicant from these nationalities (Afghanistan, Algeria, Bahrain, Bangladesh, Belarus, Cameroon, Egypt, Indonesia, Iraq, Iran, Jordan, Kenya, Korea, Kuwait, Lebanon, Libya, Mali, Morocco, Nigeria, Oman, Pakistan, Qatar, Russia, Saudi Arabia, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria, Tunisia, Yemen) applies for a C or D visa needs to provide a questionnaire while applying at our Visa application center.
Please download the questionnaire form

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Photograph attached (paper clipped) to the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements, then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the photo booth in the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
The minimum processing time of a visa application is 15 working days, so applicants are advised to leave enough time for the Embassy to process their visa application; no guarantees can be given on the visa application’s return date.
The processing of a visa application from the citizens of countries of which an additional assessment is performed can take up to 60 days. The list of countries is available here: Noteikumi par Latvijas Republikas diplomātisko un konsulāro pārstāvniecību teritoriālo kompetenci vīzu pieprasīšanai. (https://m.likumi.lv/ta/en/en/id/212364)
It is advisable to plan your travel well in advance to avoid any inconvenience. The Embassy will not process visa applications submitted late as a priority.

## Download Form
For Latvia, every applicant needs to provide a signed printout of the Long stay visa application form that has to be completed online (https://epak.pmlp.gov.lv/NVIS.EService001.WebSite/).
For required document, please check the checklist.

visa_types.name: Short Stay Visa
visa_types.overview:
Schengen visa
Citizens of some non-EU countries are required to hold a valid Schengen visa when travelling to the Schengen area, which is a group of European countries that allows a person, who’s been admitted
                                                            to the Schengen Area, to travel without any further form of border control within the Schengen Area.
Current Schengen States are: Austria, Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, the Netherlands,
                                                            Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden and Switzerland.
A Schengen visa is a short stay visa, i.e. for stays up to 90 days, allowing its holder to circulate in the Schengen Area.
A Schengen visa has to be obtained from the Embassy of the Schengen State, which will be the main destination (i.e. where the most nights will be spent) during a single trip to the Schengen Area.
A single trip is considered when an applicant enters the Schengen Area, stays there for a certain period and eventually leaves the Schengen Area.
If an equal amount of nights will be spent in different Schengen States during a single trip to the Schengen Area, then the Schengen visa must be obtained with the Embassy of the Schengen State,
                                                            which will be entered first.
The documentation related to the means of travel and accommodation must cover the complete itinerary of the single trip to the Schengen Area; i.e. travel tickets must be provided, not only for the
                                                            travel from and to the Schengen Area, but also for the travel between the different Schengen States, when more than 1 Schengen State will be visited during the single trip.
Each Schengen visa application must fully comply with the documents required for the respective visa category and can only be submitted up to 6 months before the intended departure date.
The acceptance of a Schengen visa application and supporting documents by VFS does not guarantee the issuance of a Schengen visa.
The issuance of a Schengen visa, the Schengen visa validity period, the number of entries and amount of travel days are at the sole discretion of the Embassy.
If a visa application is returned by the Embassy requesting missing documents or any other missing/ incorrect information, the applicant must accept responsibility for any delay that may occur in
                                                            the visa application processing time.
Holders of valid Schengen visas are still subject to immigration control upon arrival in the Schengen Area and are not necessarily guaranteed entry into any of the Schengen countries, even though
                                                            they may hold a valid Schengen visa for these countries.

## Visa Fees

[Table:  Tourism]
- Applicable visa fee, in EGP (to be paid in cash, credit card or debit card), is as per the current exchange rate and may be subject to change without notice.
- VFS service fee of EGP 1874 (VAT tax included) is charged per visa application, in addition to the applicable visa fee.

- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
The list of documents below is not an exhaustive list of documents and the applicant can always be asked to provide additional information/documents, if requested by the Embassy.
Both the standard and the supporting documentation have to be recent, i.e. not older than 1 month, and need to be provided for every Schengen visa application.
Applicants always have to bring and present their original documents, but they only need to submit photocopies of the same; unless the requirements specify that an original document has to be submitted.
If the applicant is under the age of 18, one of the Parents must at least accompany the minor during the visit at VFS Center, and the other parent must provide an original official (Notarized) consent letter in both English and Arabic. also, if one of the applicants is living abroad must provide an original official (Notarized) consent letter in both English and Arabic. please note that original birth certificate translated into English must be provided and provide copies from parent's passports.
Documents to be provided by all visa applicants must be in English.
The list of documents is available here: CHECKLIST
Please note:
- Only applications with all of the supporting documents will be accepted.
- Each applicant must present a separate application form and set of the supporting documents.
- Documents to be provided by all visa applicants must be in English.
For Latvia, every applicant from these nationalities (Afghanistan, Algeria, Bahrain, Bangladesh, Belarus, Cameroon, Egypt, Indonesia, Iraq, Iran, Jordan, Kenya, Korea, Kuwait, Lebanon, Libya, Mali, Morocco, Nigeria, Oman, Pakistan, Qatar, Russia, Saudi Arabia, Somalia, South Sudan, Sudan, Syria, Tunisia, Yemen) applies for a C or D visa needs to provide a questionnaire while applying at our Visa application center.
Please download the questionnaire form

## Photo Specifications
Please provide one recent colour photograph (not more than 6 months old) of yourself, meeting the following requirements:
- Taken against a light (white or off-white) background so that features are distinguishable and contrast against the background.
- Clear quality and with the face in focus
- Full face: non-smiling, no sunglasses, no hat/cap or any head covering, unless the applicant wears such items because of his/her religious belief or ethnic background
- Printed on normal photographic paper (camera print)
- Photograph attached (paper clipped) to the Visa Application Form
If the photographs presented do not meet these requirements, then the visa application will be considered incomplete and applicant will be asked to take new photographs in the
                                                                                photo booth in the visa application centre.

## Processing Time
The minimum processing time of a visa application is 15 working days, so applicants are advised to leave enough time for the Embassy to process their visa application; no guarantees can be given on the visa application’s return date.

For Latvia, every applicant needs to provide a signed printout of the Schengen visa application form that has to be completed online (https://epak.pmlp.gov.lv/NVIS.EService001.WebSite/).
For required document, please check the checklist.
